title: Different Doors
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 40 participants performing a two-door decision-making task with monetary feedback. Participants completed 20 blocks of 20 trials each, alternating between learnable blocks where one door had a 60% win probability and the other a 10% win probability, and unlearnable blocks where outcomes were randomized. Post-block surveys assessed subjective perceptions of door quality, enjoyment, motivation, and performance. The dataset enables investigation of neural correlates of learning, decision-making, and feedback processing under conditions of varying environmental predictability.
methods_description: EEG data were collected while participants performed a two-door choice task with monetary feedback. Blocks alternated between learnable (60% vs 10% win probabilities) and unlearnable (randomized outcomes) conditions. Each block contained 20 trials followed by a brief survey assessing subjective impressions of door quality, enjoyment, motivation, and performance.
